source: The estimate that the eruption darkened the northern sky for eighteen months[12] rests on tree rings, ice cores, and a handful of chronicles whose authors had no idea they were describing the same event. Each source is weak on its own. The rings give a year but not a season, the cores give a chemistry but not a place, and the chronicles give a vividness that historians have learned to distrust precisely because it is vivid. Together they converge, which is the only kind of proof this sort of question admits.

expect.why: A bracketed endnote number has no spoken form.

prepared: The estimate that the eruption darkened the northern sky for eighteen months rests on tree rings, ice cores, and a handful of chronicles whose authors had no idea they were describing the same event. Together they converge, which is the only kind of proof this sort of question admits.
notes: A numeric reference marker fused to the preceding word with no space either side. The anchor is four characters and the surrounding words must be untouched; a model that anchors “months[12]” and replaces it with “months” is also right, but one that deletes the duration is not.
